# Project Solaris — Technology Stack

The core innovation of Project Solaris is a tandem perovskite-silicon cell
architecture. This pairs a perovskite top layer (which captures high-energy
photons) with a silicon bottom layer (which captures lower-energy photons).

Key technical advantages:
- Higher theoretical efficiency ceiling than single-junction silicon
- Lower manufacturing temperatures than traditional silicon-only cells
- Potential for flexible and semi-transparent modules

Initial prototypes achieved 28.4% efficiency under standard AM1.5G test
conditions as of late 2024.
